methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official programme, newsletter, and publication pages, but no binding AI-use policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsiuniversity.edu.my UCSI University library copyright generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official thesis/dissertation guidelines PDF with a generative AI declaration requirement.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ucsiuniversity.edu.my UCSI University academic integrity plagiarism student handbook
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official undergraduate and postgraduate handbooks, a thesis guideline PDF, and a UCSI library Turnitin page.

Direct robots.txt HEAD requests to www.ucsiuniversity.edu.my and lib.ucsiuniversity.edu.my returned Cloudflare challenge responses from this environment. Firecrawl fetches of specific official URLs succeeded; no access controls were bypassed.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found UCSI thesis/dissertation guidelines requiring postgraduate candidates to disclose generative AI use and avoid using generative AI to replace core academic work or entering confidential/sensitive data in third-party AI tools. Official student handbooks and a library page support narrow plagiarism, examination-integrity, and Turnitin tool claims. Discovery did not find a public UCSI-wide generative-AI policy, approved-tool list for AI systems, or comprehensive AI teaching/assessment policy.

targets.sourceTitle: Thesis & Dissertation Guidelines for Postgraduate Candidates
Student Handbook (Procedures & Guidelines) for Undergraduate Programmes

stopConditions: Stop after official UCSI sources support narrow claim candidates.
Do not infer a university-wide AI policy from postgraduate thesis formatting guidance.
Do not treat AI programme pages, facilities pages, newsletters, research profiles, or curriculum descriptions as binding AI-use policy.

citation.citationTitle: Thesis & Dissertation Guidelines for Postgraduate Candidates

artifacts.claimText: UCSI's postgraduate handbook treats repeated plagiarism as an academic-integrity offence that can lead to fail grades, suspension, and termination after repeated offences.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Where a student is found to have committed the plagiarism offence again... The penalty for such level of plagiarism, if proven with documented evidence is automatic Fail grade in that module... When a student is caught plagiarizing after the third time... Your study at UCSI University will be terminated.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a general postgraduate academic-integrity claim. It is not AI-specific.

artifacts.claimText: UCSI's Tan Sri Musa Mohamad Library provides Turnitin as an academic tool for plagiarism detection, citation practices, and encouraging authentic student work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Turnitin Guideline | Tan Sri Musa Mohamad Library
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Turnitin is a valuable academic tool provided by the Tan Sri Musa Mohamad Library to uphold integrity and originality in academic work. It is used to detect plagiarism, ensure proper citation practices, and encourage students to produce authentic content.

citation.citationTitle: Turnitin Guideline | Tan Sri Musa Mohamad Library
citation.publisher: Tan Sri Musa Mohamad Library, UCSI University

artifacts.notes: Approved as a library academic-integrity tool claim. It does not imply Turnitin is an approved AI tool.

artifacts.claimText: UCSI's postgraduate handbook states that electronic devices are prohibited items in the examination hall, and that open-book examinations do not include internet or computer-device access.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Prohibited Items... Electronic devices (smart watch, Bluetooth hand-free)... Open book exams are meant for open book format, but do not cover access for internet or computer devices.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a postgraduate exam-integrity claim. It is not a generative-AI policy claim.

artifacts.claimText: UCSI's undergraduate handbook states that students must comply with handbook rules and that electronic devices are prohibited items in final examinations unless the practical examination requires computers.

citation.citationTitle: Student Handbook (Procedures & Guidelines) for Undergraduate Programmes

artifacts.sourceTitle: Student Handbook (Procedures & Guidelines) for Undergraduate Programmes
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: To understand and comply with all the rules, regulations, procedures and guidelines stated in the Student Handbook... Prohibited Items... Electronic devices... Open book exams are meant for open book format, but do not cover access for internet or computer devices.

citation.citationTitle: Student Handbook (Procedures & Guidelines) for Undergraduate Programmes

artifacts.notes: Approved as an undergraduate exam-integrity claim, scoped to the handbook text and not to AI-specific policy.

artifacts.title: UCSI University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Official-source crawl found a postgraduate thesis/dissertation guideline requiring disclosure of generative AI use, advising against using generative AI to replace original academic work, and prohibiting entry of confidential or sensitive data into third-party AI tools. Additional official UCSI sources support general plagiarism, Turnitin, and exam-integrity claims. No public UCSI-wide generative-AI policy, AI approved-tool list, or comprehensive AI teaching/assessment policy was found during this run.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

Direct HTTP requests from this environment to UCSI domains returned Cloudflare challenge responses; Firecrawl was used for official-source extraction without bypassing login, CAPTCHA, paywall, or other access controls.
The AI-specific claims are scoped to postgraduate thesis/dissertation preparation; this run did not find public official evidence for a UCSI-wide generative-AI policy.
